discrepancies.discovered_during: umbrella_v22.pdf locate request; PDF substrate sanity-check vs Zenodo description block
discrepancies.claim: The PDF's own UMB-row entry in the §6 companion-papers table cites '10.5281/zenodo.19885550' as the umbrella concept-DOI.
discrepancies.evidence: Line 851 verbatim: 'concept \href{https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19885550}{10.5281/zenodo.19885550} (v1, v2.0 superseded; v2.1 not deposited)'
discrepancies.contradicts: Slot 163-164 verdict (bridge SHA b936eb0) which propagated concept-DOI correction 19885550 -> 19885549 throughout slot 167 deposit substrate. Slot 167 amended_description_block_v2.md §1 metadata correctly cites concept 19885549 / v1.0 version 19885550. Live Zenodo verification: 19885550 is the v1.0 VERSION-DOI; 19885549 is the CONCEPT-DOI.
discrepancies.impact: The PDF body uploaded to Zenodo v2.2 will contain an internally-inconsistent self-citation: the Zenodo record metadata will cite concept 19885549, but the PDF's own §6 table will cite 19885550 as 'concept'. Cosmetic-tier inconsistency; does not affect machine-resolvability of related-identifiers (Zenodo uses the metadata, not the PDF body). Long-term substrate-trail hygiene argues for rebuild.

options.option_A_rebuild: Edit umbrella_v22.tex line 851 (19885550 -> 19885549; '(v1, v2.0 superseded; ...)' -> '(concept; resolves to latest)'); rebuild PDF; re-upload to Zenodo draft. Cost: ~5min LaTeX recompile + re-upload. Benefit: clean substrate-trail.
options.option_B_accept: Upload current PDF; note discrepancy in Zenodo description body or version-notes; corrigendum-via-Edit later if desired. Cost: 0. Benefit: deposit unblocked immediately.
options.option_C_corrigendum_only: Upload current PDF; defer fix to Umbrella v2.3 (next content-tier revision; not yet drafted). Cost: 0. Benefit: deposit unblocked; fix folded into next mathematical-content revision.

discrepancies.recommendation: Option_B (accept) — Zenodo's machine-readable metadata uses the related-identifiers block (correct), not the PDF body. The PDF table line is cosmetic and will be superseded when Umbrella v2.3 lands. Halt for rebuild only if operator wants pristine substrate-trail.

discrepancies.resolution_evidence:
Zenodo API GET /api/records/20114861 at 2026-05-11 ~09:52 JST returned revision=4 (up from 3), description length 4739 chars (down from 17747), opening '

v2.2 (May 2026): Consolidates full M-axis V0 closure series...' and closing '...Umbrella v2.3 (forthcoming; mathematical content amendment).

'; contamination check on 13 meta-document markers (slot 167 addendum / Parent slot / Part A / Part B / Part C / BEGIN_PASTE / paste-source / TinyMCE / End of ZENODO / Zenodo-Clean Description Body / workflow / ZENODO_CLEAN / 3f0377a) returned CLEAN/CLEAN/.../CLEAN; expected-content check on 18 umbrella markers (Full M-axis V0 Closure Series / M7 V0 / M8a V0 / M8b V0 / 7f93b9e / cb429e1 / 74c5630 / cascade-132 / 20114315 / 19972394 / 19915689 / 19937196 / b9aa881 / M1 / M12 / axis-coverage / Closure Cascade / Painlev) returned PRESENT 18/18; TinyMCE added cosmetic   around elements (no semantic impact). Fix performed by operator using ZENODO_DESCRIPTION_DROPIN_FIX.html drop-in BEGIN_PASTE/END_PASTE content per metadata-only Edit workflow; concept DOI 19885549 and version DOI 20114861 both UNCHANGED as expected.
discrepancies.discovered_during: Live Zenodo record verification immediately post-publish (umbrella v2.2 record 20114861)
discrepancies.substrate: https://zenodo.org/records/20114861 Description field (metadata.description in Zenodo API)
discrepancies.claim: Operator pasted the ENTIRE meta-document ZENODO_CLEAN_DESCRIPTION_BODY.md verbatim into the Zenodo Description field, including the file's Markdown wrapper, header (# Zenodo-Clean Description Body -- Umbrella v2.2 (slot 167 addendum)), the meta-block (**Date**: 2026-05-11 09:15 JST, **Parent slot**: 167 (bridge SHA 3f0377a)), 'Part A -- Plain-prose mirror' header, 'Part B -- TinyMCE HTML-ready mirror' header, 'Part C -- Diff log' header + 11-row table, 'Operator paste workflow' section, and trailing '**End of ZENODO_CLEAN_DESCRIPTION_BODY.md.**' line. The actual umbrella description body content (sec 2 expanded variant + sec 2.1 blockquote + sec 2.5 axis-coverage table) is present BUT buried inside code fences (```html ... ```) which TinyMCE rendered as literal text rather than as live HTML.
discrepancies.evidence:
Zenodo API GET /api/records/20114861 description field starts with '

# Zenodo-Clean Description Body — Umbrella v2.2 (slot 167 addendum)

' and ends with '

**End of `ZENODO_CLEAN_DESCRIPTION_BODY.md`.**

'. Description length 17747 chars; expected ~4500 chars (Part B HTML only).
discrepancies.root_cause: Slot 167 ZENODO_CLEAN_DESCRIPTION_BODY.md was authored as a meta-instruction document containing the paste-source rather than as a drop-in paste-source. The file's three-part structure (Part A plain prose / Part B HTML / Part C diff log) plus instructional headers required the operator to extract Part B from within the file rather than paste the whole file. Cognitive-load gap: the operator-runbook step did not explicitly say 'extract the HTML between Part B's first ```html and the closing ``` — paste only that'.
discrepancies.impact: HIGH: viewers landing on the Zenodo record see internal operational text (slot numbers, bridge SHAs, paste-workflow instructions) instead of the umbrella paper description. Cosmetic-tier on archived-record-content semantics but reputation-tier on professional presentation. The actual scientific content (PDF + .tex source) is correct and unaffected; this is a metadata-Description-field issue only.
discrepancies.fix_path: Open Zenodo record 20114861 -> Edit metadata -> Description field -> SELECT ALL -> DELETE -> source-view -> paste ONLY the content of ZENODO_DESCRIPTION_DROPIN_FIX.html (between BEGIN_PASTE and END_PASTE markers; ~4.5KB clean HTML) -> Save -> Publish. Zenodo will bump revision count (currently 3) but NOT version DOI (metadata-Edit only, per D2-NOTE v2.1 precedent at 2026-05-04).
discrepancies.recommended_action: FIX IMMEDIATELY before record gets indexed by Google Scholar / cited by external parties. Estimated 2 minutes operator time using ZENODO_DESCRIPTION_DROPIN_FIX.html as paste-source.
discrepancies.process_lesson: Future Zenodo paste-source files MUST be authored as direct drop-in replacements (single contiguous block; no meta-wrapper; no multi-part variants; no instructional preamble). If multi-variant authoring is needed for review purposes, the deliverable for the operator's paste-time use MUST be a separate '_DROPIN' artifact containing ONLY the content to paste, with paste-instructions in an HTML comment at the top of the file. ZENODO_CLEAN_DESCRIPTION_BODY.md should have produced TWO files: the review-document (which it is) AND a sibling '_DROPIN' that contains only the chosen variant's content. This memory-class lesson should propagate to future cascade fires.

discrepancies.discovered_during: umbrella_v22.pdf locate request; PDF substrate sanity-check vs Zenodo description block
discrepancies.claim: The PDF's CT-row entry in the §6 companion-papers table cites Channel Theory v1.2 (Zenodo 19951331).
discrepancies.contradicts: Zenodo description block §2.1 (verdict-166-γ2 footnote) cites Channel Theory v1.3 (19972394, 2026-05-02). Live Zenodo: CT v1.3 IS published; PDF cites the immediate predecessor v1.2 (which was published 2026-05-01 per the PDF's own date stamp).
discrepancies.impact: Same as D-167-1: cosmetic PDF-vs-Zenodo-metadata version-staleness on companion citation. Concept-DOI (19941678) is correct; only the version-DOI/version-label is stale. PDF was built 2026-05-09 19:44 JST, post-CT-v1.3-publish, so this is a stale-citation oversight not a temporal-impossibility.

options.option_A_rebuild: Edit line 849: v1.2 -> v1.3; 19951331 -> 19972394; 2026-05-01 -> 2026-05-02. Combine with D-167-1 rebuild for single recompile pass.
options.option_B_accept: Cited concept-DOI is correct; version-staleness is forgivable in a program-statement paper that explicitly versions itself separately from its companions. Accept.

discrepancies.recommendation: Option_B unless rebuilding for D-167-1 anyway (in which case fold both).

scope_note: Both D-167-1 and D-167-2 are PDF substrate vs Zenodo metadata mismatches that surfaced during pre-deposit verification. Zenodo deposit can proceed with metadata correct per slot 167; PDF body inconsistencies are cosmetic-tier and do not affect machine-resolvability. Operator-decision-tier per cascade-132 §3.4 Q4 admin-path admissibility (low-stakes).
